other_observers: Jessie Barry, Joey Kellner, Steve Stachowiak.; Found by me.
description: I was birding at Jumbo Reservoir with Jessie Barry, Joey Kellner and Steve Stachowiak. I was scanning the lake with my binocular when I spotted a very pale gull that immediately struck me as a Kumlien's Gull or an extremely pale and worn Thayer's Gull. Upon looking through the scope I realized it was an Iceland Gull and drew everyone's attention to the bird. We were able to watch the bird for about twenty minutes at a distance of ca. 150 - 400 meters. During this time I was able to get a few very distant, but identifiable images. These photos show much what needs to be seen, but I was not able to get a photo of the upperside of the bird in flight so my description will focus on that.  Overall, similar to a Thayer's Gull but slightly smaller and appeared longer-winged when resting on water with 3 1/2 primaries extending past the tip of the tail. Bill also appeared smaller. Slimmer. Overall color pattern frostier, particularly on tertials when resting on water. head white. Upperparts extensively frosty in color. Underparts fairly pale, but more brownish on belly. Some mottling with whitish and pale brown but also somewhat worn making exact pattern difficult to determine. Scapulars and tertials pale with darker pale brown barring and white notches.  In flight this big appeared very pale. From above it showed only a very faint hint of a brown wash to the secondaries--not the dark brown secondary bar shown by Thayer's Gull. Darker outer webs to primaries restricted to P9, P8 and possibly to a lesser extent P7. Overall this seemed to be an average Kumlien's--not extremely frosty or worn, but also not on the darker end. Tail slightly darker than primaries with a bit of a darker smudge subterminally and white tail tip--fairly similar to Howell and Dunn photo 35a.35. Overall color pattern of tail blended smoothly into rump without much contrast. NOTE resting photo of bird on water, tail is shaded and looks darker than it appeared at most times in the field.  Eye dark. Bill dark with a bit of flesh color coming into base of bill. Leg color not seen/noted.Behaviors: The bird was seen resting on the water alone. There were a few other gulls including two Glaucous Gulls (one appeared similar but slightly paler in overall color aspect, the other much whiter). They dwarfed this bird (but were proportionately shorter winged). Twice the bird got up, flew and circled. I was watching the bird closely in my scope the first time. The second time I got my camera and got a couple distant images. It did not interact with any other birds. On the water the bird rode slightly higher, with wings and tail held higher above water than nearby Herring Gulls (direct comparison).Call: NonePlumage: first-cycle
photographer: Photographer: Christopher Wood, Christopher Wood, Christopher Wood
